subject: Project Management
title: Wembley Stadium Rebuild
description: A fast breakdown of Wembley Stadium Rebuild for builders.

hook.text: England demolished its iconic Wembley Stadium in 2003 to build a new one. Chaos followed.

buildup.text: The project was plagued by contractor disputes, design changes, and a 133-meter steel arch nobody could agree on.

discovery.text: The Australian contractor, Multiplex, lost £100 million on the project and sued the subcontractors.

twist.text: Wembley opened in 2007 — four years late and £400 million over its £326 million budget.

climax.text: Despite the chaos, the new Wembley became a beloved national landmark. Time heals budget overruns.

punchline.text: The stadium was worth the wait. Not the bill.

quiz.question: What was a major source of conflict during Wembley's rebuild?
options: Contractor disputes and a controversial steel arch design
The old stadium refused to fall down
